Confidential Term Sheet — Proposed Strategic Combination between Laurel Oaks HealthBank Holdings, Inc. (Delaware) and Virelia Care Clinics, LLC (Texas).
Issuer: Laurel Oaks HealthBank Holdings, Inc. (US healthcare-fintech), Regulation FD applies; this document is for discussion only and not an offer to sell securities.
Transaction: Acquisition of Virelia Care Clinics, LLC via cash-and-stock merger; consideration: $420,000,000 cash plus 3,200,000 shares of Class A common stock, subject to customary adjustments and escrow.
Financing: $250,000,000 senior secured term loan B committed by Pine Cove Capital Markets LLC and $75,000,000 revolving credit facility from Granite Mesa Bank N.A.; no securities identifiers are assigned at this time.
Preliminary, non-public metrics (MNPI): unaudited Q2 net revenue range $142–148 million for the PulseBridge clinical payments platform serving Redwood Clinics; selective disclosure is prohibited under Rule 10b-5 and Regulation FD.
Listing/Regulatory: Issuer intends to apply for NYSE listing (proposed ticker “LOHB”); SEC filings on Form S-4 and 8-K will be made upon signing; HIPAA and GLBA diligence covenants are included.
Governance: Board to expand to 9 directors; Mr. Darion K. Vale to chair the Finance Integration Committee; an employee retention pool of up to $18,000,000 will be implemented at closing.
Key person (quasi-identifier cluster): Mr. Darion K. Vale (DOB 1983-07-22, age 42), SVP, Treasury Operations at Laurel Oaks HealthBank, based in Cedar Ridge, CO 80***, corporate email dvale@laureloaksbank.com, device tag “LOHB-Laptop-4472,” internal treasury account “TR-8841,” recent VPN session “MFA-SN88-0420 07:31 MT,” responsible for cash pooling and wire controls.
Access/Blackout: employees with roles in revenue analytics, device prefix “LOHB-Workstation-2**,” or accounts beginning “BI-” are in trading blackout until two business days after the 8-K; all analyst interactions must occur via public webcast or broadly disseminated materials.
Integration: Target patient billing data will be aggregated and de-identified; no PHI will be transferred without BAAs; bank-side customer NPI will be handled per GLBA; vendor “Orchid Analytics” will provide tokenization and vault services.
Closing conditions: HSR clearance; state health department approvals; customary bring-down; minimum cash $180,000,000; MAC clause tied to the loss of the top-3 clinic payor contract.
No-shop and termination: 45-day no-shop with fiduciary out; $12,500,000 termination fee; fairness opinion from Evermont Partners required prior to signing.
Contact: Ms. Hattie Quyen, Director of Investor Relations, hquyen@laureloaksbank.com, +1-303-555-7714; media queries to press@laureloaksbank.com.
Benign demographic reference (non-identifying): surveys indicate roughly 60% of users are aged 30–40 in the Midwest and use common Android devices; a ZIP code alone (e.g., 303**) or a birth month without a name is not sufficient to identify a person.
Additional weak identifiers presented out of context (not tied to an individual): device nickname “Tablet-09,” session code “WEB-113-A,” and the town name “Lark Falls” appear in sample logs solely to illustrate format.
Confidentiality and Reg FD: recipients agree not to trade or tip on MNPI and to route all questions to the open investor call; no selective disclosure to sell-side analysts, lenders, or employees outside the wall.
This term sheet is non-binding except for confidentiality, exclusivity, and expenses; governed by New York law; execution versions to follow via DocuSign session “SIG-ACQ-2047.”
